research.title: useOptimistic in React 19: Instant UI with Server Actions and Automatic Rollback
research.whyTrending: User-requested. React 19 made useOptimistic stable; combined with server actions it delivers instant UI feedback without API routes, and the rollback-on-error behavior is widely misunderstood.

seo.suggestedTitle: useOptimistic in React 19: Instant UI That Rolls Itself Back

gaps: Most demos are a like-button; no list CRUD with temp IDs and reconciliation
Rollback semantics explained hand-wavily; nobody explains WHY state reverts (rebase-on-base-state model)
The startTransition requirement and the outside-transition warning rarely covered
No error surfacing pattern after rollback (pairing with useActionState)
Race conditions with multiple in-flight actions not covered
strengths: Epic React explains the mental model well

expertInsights: useOptimistic state = base state + pending optimistic updates replayed; when all transitions settle, React re-renders from base state — that IS the automatic rollback
addOptimistic must be called inside a transition (or form action); calling it outside warns and flickers
The update function must be pure — no side effects, no validation inside the reducer
Pair with useActionState for error display after rollback
Temp IDs needed for optimistic list inserts; server response reconciles via revalidation
GitHub issue #31967: unexpected rollbacks when state updates happen outside transitions
commonProblems: Optimistic update flickers/reverts because setState ran outside the transition
Keying optimistic items by index instead of temp ID

strategy.angle: Explain the rebase mental model that makes rollback obvious (optimistic state is derived, replayed on top of base state), then build a real task list with temp IDs, error surfacing via useActionState, and multiple in-flight actions — not another like button
strategy.title: useOptimistic in React 19: Instant UI With Automatic Rollback
strategy.excerpt: useOptimistic is not setState with a twist — it is derived state replayed on top of the last confirmed value. Once you get that model, automatic rollback stops being magic. A practical guide with server actions, temp IDs, and error handling.
hooks: Contrarian: rollback is not a feature bolted on — it falls out of the data model
Gotcha: calling addOptimistic outside a transition is the #1 cause of flickering
Practical: like-button demos hide the hard parts — lists, temp IDs, races
outline: Hook: the update that reverted for no reason
The mental model: optimistic state is derived, not set
A real example: optimistic task list with temp IDs
Rollback and error surfacing with useActionState
When NOT to use optimistic UI
